CSP App · Exit + CI + Enforcement · v1

Exit + CI + Enforcement — Design Portal

What the CSP sees across the back half of the partner lifecycle — recovery, enforcement standing, and exit — with every component, its data source, the decisions behind it, and the full backend & frontend dependency map. One tab per feature module.

Enforcement OS v1.10.1 · CI OS v1.6.1 · Exit OS v1.11.1 (LOCKED) M1 + M2 built & verified on emulator
Overview Mental model Home banner Drilldown Data flow Backend deps Variables audit Frontend deps Deeplinking Decisions Critical & gaps

0 What this is

When a CSP is struggling, the platform doesn't punish — it runs a bounded recovery programme. The actual help (coaching, training, field support) happens off-app; the app's only job is to explain the plan, show what's changed while recovery is underway, and make the support visible. Two surfaces carry this: a Home banner (entry) and a detail screen (the explainer). Everything is server-driven — the app renders, it composes nothing.

OS-CITED — fixed by a locked OS DESIGN-OWNED — OS silent, our call PRODUCT — team surface (Assurance Strip) FIELD-VOC — field learnings, directional

1 The one thing to internalise

Across the CSP lifecycle, money has a direction. On the Enforcement and Exit stages money moves away from the CSP (bonus pause, freeze, deduction, settlement). In Capability Intervention it moves toward them — a capped support credit. The whole experience must read as help, not penalty.

Quality OSmeasures the truth→ Enforcementdecides posture→ Capability Interventionattempts recovery · YOU ARE HERE→ Exitends participation

CI starts only on Enforcement's CAPABILITY_RESET_TRIGGERED and ends in exactly one of three terminal outcomes: recovery (success → back to normal), exit recommended (→ Exit B1), or voluntary exit mid-plan (→ Exit, a constitutional right). OS · §4.2

2 Home banner — the entry point

The banner is the only thing a CSP needs to discover the programme. It is the bridge from Home into the plan. Reused from an existing repo label — not newly written.

Home · CI active scenario

Banner copy product pre-existing label

सुधार कार्यक्रम चालू — Wiom मदद करेगा
नया काम अभी थोड़ा कम मिलेगा

Title
banner.ci_active
Subtitle
banner.ci_active.subtitle
Severity
INFO → lavender, ⓘ icon, left accent bar (deliberately not WARNING/CRITICAL — help, not alarm)
Tap
tap_action: NAVIGATE → tap_target.route: "ci_detail/CI-2026-0042"
Dismissible
No — a live programme can't be swiped away
Priority
P1 — top of the banner stack while CI is active
Gating
shown only when ci_intervention != null in /home/feed
Provenance: this copy already existed in home_labels_hi_en.json — it was reused, not authored for M1. It already fits Wiom voice: positive ("सुधार कार्यक्रम"), benefit-framed ("Wiom मदद करेगा"), honest about the taper without fear.

3 The drilldown — सुधार कार्यक्रम · #0042

Four zones, top to bottom: State (what & why), Wiom's help (interventions + support credit), What you must do (recovery actions), What happens next (outcomes). Plus a human-contact footer. Every string is server-rendered; the detail comes from GET /ci/detail/{id}.

CI Detail · CiDetailScreen.kt

Header — सुधार कार्यक्रम · #0042 os-cited

Means
"Recovery Programme" + last-4 of the intervention id
Data
ci.screen.title + interventionId.takeLast(4)
Disclosure ceiling: only the id suffix shows — never the internal risk class (R0/R1/R2), scores, or thresholds. OS §4.5

Zone A — स्थिति (State) os-cited

शुरू
plan start date — startDateDisplay · a date, never a countdown / cycle count
वजह
the cause (tappable, pink) — triggerReasonDisplay → onQualityLink cross-links to the Quality chip
Taper line
routingTaperDisplay — encodes 3 facts: zone-scoped, new connections only, temporary
Copy flag: the word "स्कोर" (score) is borderline against the disclosure rule — we may say quality is below the line (functional standing) but never the number. Settle wording with copy/ops.

Zone B — Wiom की मदद (Support) os-cited

Intervention rows
✓ each off-app help item — interventionTypes[].display
Support box
सहायता / अब तक / सीमा = per-recharge, total disbursed, cap — financialSupport.* (shown only when eligible == true)
The spine of the feature: money moves toward the CSP here. The ₹ values are placeholders — OS caps the credit below compliant bonus (P103/P104), computed by Compensation, and they must be server-supplied, never hardcoded.

Zone C — आपको क्या करना है (Actions) os-cited

Bullets
concrete recovery actions — cspActionItems[] (0..n, server-supplied)
Enablement-first: "how to recover", never scolding.

Zone D — आगे क्या होगा (Outcomes) os-cited

✓ Recovery
recoveryOutcomeDisplay — the path back, always achievable
✗ Failure
failureOutcomeDisplay — deliberately vague
Correct by design: failure copy never says "exit/termination" (PRE_EXIT is invisible) and promises they'll be told first — contestability before finality.

Footer — Wiom सपोर्ट से बात करें design-owned

Means
"Talk to Wiom support" — ci.support.call_link → onSupportLink
Human channel — nearest thing to the safety break-glass. True break-glass (self-harm → named human, same hour) is ops-owned, flagged as a dependency.
Relapse (hidden here): when isRelapse == true a red relapseDisplay line shows at the top of Zone A. Bounded rehabilitation = max 2 relapses (P_CI_MAX_RELAPSES), but the screen must never show the relapse counter — only a supportive server string.

4 How data reaches the screen

GET /home/feedci_intervention block→ CI bannergated on != null→ tap CiDetailViewModelreads interventionId→ GET /ci/detail/{id}full plan→ CiDetailDto → CiDetailDatamapper→ CiDetailScreenrenders

The feed block is intentionally minimal (id + state + deeplink) so the feed stays light — it only needs enough to gate the banner. The heavy plan detail is a separate fetch, so the detail screen shows the feed's minimal data instantly (header) and swaps in the full plan once /ci/detail returns. BACKEND_CI_SPEC §4

5 Backend dependencies

Everything is backend-driven with safe defaults — no surface renders until the backend opts in, so production is unaffected until CI ships server-side.

Endpoint / fieldPayloadPurposeStatus
GET /home/feed → ci_intervention{ ci_state, intervention_id, ci_detail_deeplink } · null when inactiveGates the banner + CI links in Quality/Earnings drilldownsfield existed
GET /ci/detail/{intervention_id}full CiDetailDto (see below)Populates the CI Detail screenNEW · M1
Wallet ledger INTERVENTION_CREDITline item carrying intervention_idMakes the support credit visible in the wallet (Compensation → Payment)existed
Push page_name = CI_DETAILexecution_id = intervention_idDeeplink from a notification straight to the planNEW · M1

CiDetailDto — the /ci/detail/{id} contract

data class CiDetailDto(
  intervention_id: String,            // echoes the path id
  ci_state: String,                   // INTERVENTION_ACTIVE | RECOVERY_CONFIRMED | CI_EXIT_RECOMMENDED | CI_TERMINATED_BY_EXIT
  start_date_display: String,         // server-rendered Hindi date
  trigger_reason_display: String,     // the cause (category, not a number)
  routing_taper_display: String,      // zone-scoped, new-connections-only, temporary
  intervention_types: [ { type, display, status } ],
  financial_support: { eligible, per_recharge_amount, total_disbursed, cap },
  csp_action_items: [ String ],
  recovery_outcome_display: String,
  failure_outcome_display: String,    // must NOT name exit/termination
  is_relapse: Boolean,
  relapse_display: String?            // supportive string; never a counter
)

6 Variables & hardcoded-value audit

Per the design note, every parameter must be read as a parameter, never hard-coded. These are the values the surface depends on — all should originate server-side; the mock supplies placeholders.

VariableMeaningDefault / mock valueOwner
P102Routing taper rate (zone-scoped, new assignments only)30% of normal new-assignment rateD&A OS
P103Support credit factor0.6Compensation
P104Support credit hard ceiling (always below compliant bonus)0.7Compensation
P106Consecutive COMPLIANT cycles to confirm recoveryOS param (not shown to CSP)CI OS
P_CI_MAX_RELAPSESRelapse cap before exit is recommended2CI OS
P108 / P_CI_RELAPSE_MEMORY_WINDOWRelapse window / clean-slate resetOS paramCI OS
per_recharge_amount 🔶Support per recharge — mock placeholder₹18Compensation (server)
total_disbursed 🔶Support disbursed so far — mock placeholder₹126Payment (server)
cap 🔶Support cap — mock placeholder₹300Compensation (server)
🔶 The three ₹ values are the only hardcoded numbers on the surface, and they live in mock JSON only. In production they are computed by Compensation/Payment and returned by /ci/detail. The app formats but never derives them.

AssetRole

Debug panel · "CI Active" toggle

How to demo

Bug icon (header) → Home Debug Panel → Install State Scenarios → CI Active → Apply & Return to Home. The banner appears → tap → CI Detail. The "CI Active" chip writes taskFeedComposition = ci_active, serving feed__ci_active.json — identical mechanism to NBREC/Shifting scenarios. Debug-only, stripped from release.

8 Deeplinking — the backend owns the target

The app honours whatever the backend emits and routes it to the CI Detail screen. Three independent paths, all converging on ci_detail/{interventionId}:

SourceWhat backend sendsHow it routes
Home banner taptap_target.route = "ci_detail/CI-2026-0042"onBannerTap → navController.navigate(route) · verified
Feed fieldci_detail_deeplink = "/ci/detail/CI-2026-0042"canonical server deeplink string (banner route derives from it)
Push notificationpage_name = CI_DETAIL, execution_id = CI-2026-0042openPage() → nav signal → CI Detail (mirrors wallet/recharge)
Key point: the specific intervention id is decided by the backend; the app never constructs it. This keeps the "information & deeplinking to the specific page handled in the backend".

9 Design decisions

#DecisionChosenWhy
D2CI plan explainer formReuse the native CI Detail screenCheaper, offline-friendly, consistent — vs. an embedded web page
D8Data source for v1Mock-first + safe-default DTOsMatches house style; production unaffected until backend ships
D9Deeplink mechanismRoute-string + CtPageName caseOne target, three server-driven entry paths
—Feed payload weightMinimal block in feed; full detail separateKeeps /home/feed light; instant header + lazy detail

10 Critical points & known gaps

🔴 Open copy decision — "स्कोर"

The trigger-reason wording names "score". Disclosure rule lets us state quality is below the line (functional standing) but never the number. Final wording to be settled with copy/ops (Copy Agent).

🔴 Hardcoded ₹ values

per-recharge / total / cap are mock placeholders only. Must be Compensation-computed and server-returned; the cap must always sit below compliant bonus (P103/P104).

🟠 Missing: voluntary-exit affordance

CI·6 requires a neutral "leave during a plan" option (constitutional right P10). Deferred to the Exit front-half (M4) so the exit experience is built coherently in one pass.

🟠 Safety break-glass is ops-owned

The app offers a human-contact footer, but real self-harm escalation (named human, same hour, outside automation) needs an ops/OS decision — flagged as a dependency, not solvable in-app alone.

🟢 Disclosure ceiling held

No scores, cycle counts, thresholds, risk class, or relapse counters are exposed anywhere on the surface.

🟢 No existing code affected

Every change is additive — a new field/route/enum-case/branch gated on new state. feed.json still returns ci_intervention: null; default behaviour unchanged.

+ §4.1 affected surfaces — full coverage

All six §4.1 surfaces now land. CI·1/CI·4/CI·5 were already covered (banner→detail, quality recovery link, earnings recovery-support block); CI·2, CI·3, CI·6 were the legibility gaps just closed.

#SurfaceStatus
CI·1In-app plan banner → detail page✅ covered
CI·2Connection State — new-connection taper🟢 added
CI·3Active connections — not changed🟢 added
CI·4Quality State — recovery progress link✅ covered
CI·5Wallet/earnings — recovery-support credit✅ covered
CI·6Voluntary-exit affordance → Exit🟢 added
CI·2 + CI·3 · Connections drilldown (CI Active)
CI·1 detail + CI·6 affordance (foot)
CI·6 → Exit consequences (double opt-in)

CI·2 — taper on the connection surface os-cited

The amber note shows the zone-scoped new-connection taper ("सेक्टर 8 में नए कनेक्शन कुछ समय के लिए कम — रिकवरी के बाद वापस सामान्य"). Server-rendered, carried on the feed's ci_intervention.routing_taper_display so the surface stays render-only.

CI·3 — "existing unaffected" reassurance design-owned

"आपके मौजूदा कनेक्शन इससे प्रभावित नहीं होंगे" — a fixed legibility line so a CSP never reads the taper as losing existing customers. Tapping the note opens the recovery plan.

CI·6 — voluntary-exit affordance os-cited · P10

A neutral, understated "बाहर निकलने का विकल्प →" at the foot of the CI detail page routes into the Exit initiation flow (double opt-in). Lands on the Exit branch (PR #447), where the exit route lives — leaving is an always-available right, never nudged.

Debug: all CI surfaces are driven by the existing "CI Active" debug chip (Home Debug Panel → Install State Scenarios) — selecting it loads the CI feed + taper + banner. No separate toggle.
Overview Banner Strip effects Backend deps Variables Frontend deps Debug Decisions Critical & gaps

0 Enforcement — the performance RESTRICTED posture

Enforcement runs as two independent machines: a performance track (reactive to Quality — bonus pause & routing taper) and an integrity/FPV track (hold → freeze → terminate, with appeal). M2 ships the performance track's RESTRICTED legibility only — read-only, no contest (there is no appeal on the performance track). The integrity/FPV flow is the deferred M3.

Money direction is the opposite of CI: on enforcement it moves away from the CSP (bonus paused). The governing rule of this surface is "the CSP sees the cause before the consequence" — Quality AT_RISK is the early warning; a restriction landing as a surprise is a design failure. So every restriction readout is paired with the path back, framed as enablement, never threat.

Decision D3: RESTRICTED is surfaced through the existing chip effects (Earnings bonus-pause, Connection-State taper) + a banner — no new posture chip. One backend posture drives all three surfaces.

1 Home banner — MB-ENF-001

Home · RESTRICTED posture

Banner copy design-owned · mock

इस महीने बोनस रुका है — सुधारें और वापस पाएं
base कमाई और निकासी जारी · नया काम थोड़ा कम

Title
banner.restricted.performance
Subtitle
banner.restricted.performance.subtitle
Severity
WARNING → amber, ⚠ icon (a caution, not an alarm)
Tap
NAVIGATE → Quality chip = the enablement / "what to improve" surface
Block
MB-ENF-001 (entry). Channel floor: in-app banner + 1 push
Four facts in two lines: what changed (bonus paused), reassurance (base + withdrawals continue), the taper (fewer new jobs), and the path back ("सुधारें और वापस पाएं"). No threat, no appeal — exactly the OS content rule for restriction.

Restriction lifted — MB-ENF-003

Copy
बोनस फिर से चालू — सब वापस सामान्य
Keys
banner.restriction_lifted (+ subtitle)
Severity
INFO, dismissible — auto-clears on return to compliance, no probation

2 Strip effects — where RESTRICTED lands

Two readouts change; the wallet stays ACTIVE (performance never freezes money — only bonus accrual pauses).

Earnings · bonus paused

Earnings — bonus paused os-cited reused

⚠ Bonus रुका है — base कमाई जारी है

Source
comp_state = "BONUS_SUSPENDED" → CompStateBanner → earnings.compState.bonusSuspended
Effect
बोनस ₹0 this window; base (₹2,500) + withdrawals continue
Placement
refined the notice lives inside the open Bonus section (which auto-expands when paused), right above "बोनस कब और कैसे आता है?" — not floating at the drilldown top
Must-do: distinguish "bonus paused" from "base pay" so the headline number never reads as lost income. The cause now sits with the ₹0 it explains. Other comp-states (financial-hold, payout-frozen) still surface at the top — they affect the whole wallet, not just bonus.
Connection-State · routing taper

Connection-State — routing taper os-cited NEW · M2

⚠ नया काम थोड़ा कम · परफ़ॉर्मेंस की वजह से… सुधरते ही वापस सामान्य · देखें क्या सुधारना है →

Source
new "RESTRICTED" branch in ExposureDrilldown · exposure.eligibility.restricted.*
Colour
Amber (AT_RISK icon) — a reduction
Critical distinction: the taper is a reduction of new connections, NOT a hard stop. SUSPENDED (integrity/FPV, routing=0) is the red hard-stop. Designing them the same would misrepresent the system — so taper is amber + carries the enablement link; the existing base count is untouched.

3 Backend dependencies

One backend posture drives three surfaces. All are server-driven with safe defaults; nothing renders until the backend emits the posture.

SurfaceField / sourceRESTRICTED value
Home banner/home/feed banner blockbanner_type=enforcement_restricted, WARNING, tap → quality
Earnings bonus pause/assurance/earnings → comp_stateBONUS_SUSPENDED
Connection-State taper/assurance/exposure → eligibilityRESTRICTED
Push (MB-ENF-001 floor)CleverTap push1 push alongside the in-app banner · backend dependency
Coherence is the backend's job. In production a single Enforcement posture emits all three. The app never infers RESTRICTED from one signal — it renders exactly what each endpoint reports.

4 Variables & states

ValueMeaningNotes
comp_stateCompensation posture on EarningsFULL / BONUS_SUSPENDED / FINANCIAL_HOLD / PAYOUT_FROZEN
eligibilityConnection-State on ExposureAUTHORIZED / RESTRICTED (taper) / SUSPENDED (hard-stop) / FROZEN_* / REVOKED
Walletstays ACTIVEperformance never freezes money — withdrawals open
P15 / AT_RISKearly-warning before restrictionQuality readout — the no-surprise surface
PATTERN_WATCH / PRE_EXITinternal enforcement statesnever surfaced — invisible at the enforcement layer (disclosure ceiling)
No hardcoded numbers on this surface — RESTRICTED carries no scores, counts, or thresholds, only functional standing + the path back. Disclosure ceiling held.

5 Frontend dependencies

FileChange
ExposureDrilldown.kt+ new RESTRICTED taper branch (amber, enablement link). Existing branches untouched.
EarningsDrilldown.ktrefined — moved the BONUS_SUSPENDED notice into the (auto-expanded) Bonus section; other comp-states unchanged
home_labels_hi_en.json+ 4 banner strings (restricted + lifted)
assurance_strip_labels…json+ 2 taper strings
HomeViewModel.kt+ applyEnforcementPosture() — one-tap combined posture
mocks (home/)+ feed__restricted, feed__restriction_lifted, earnings__bonus_suspended, exposure__restricted
Home + Assurance debug screens+ scenario chips + the one-tap posture section

6 Debug — one-tap posture

Assurance Debug · Enforcement Posture (one-tap)

Enforcement Posture (one-tap)

Because the full posture spans three endpoints, a single control sets them coherently:

Normal
clears all three → baseline
Restricted (full)
banner + bonus pause + routing taper, all at once
Restriction Lifted
MB-ENF-003 banner, strip back to normal

Backed by applyEnforcementPosture(), which writes taskFeedComposition + earningsScenario + exposureScenario together and refreshes feed + strip. Granular per-knob chips remain for isolated testing.

7 Design decisions

#DecisionChosenWhy
D3Where posture livesExisting chip effects + banner — no new chipAvoids a new surface; matches how the OS maps effects onto readouts
—Taper vs freeze visualAmber taper, distinct from red SUSPENDEDReduction ≠ hard stop — the difference is load-bearing
—Banner tap targetQuality chip (enablement)Performance has no appeal — offer "how to improve", not a contest
—Debug ergonomicsOne-tap combined postureThree endpoints, one backend posture — demo it as one

8 Critical points & gaps

🔴 Banner/taper copy is mock

All RESTRICTED strings I wrote are placeholders pending the Copy Agent. Framing follows OS rules (factual, enablement-first, path-back, no threat).

🟠 "1 push" is a backend dependency

MB-ENF-001's channel floor is in-app banner + 1 push. The push is emitted by CleverTap server-side; the app handles the tap target.

🟠 Integrity / FPV deferred (M3)

Hold → freeze → terminate, hold≠deduction, the single appeal/contest, direct-contact — all the heavy greenfield — is the deferred M3.

🟢 Disclosure ceiling held

No scores, window counts, PATTERN_WATCH, or PRE_EXIT exposed. Only functional standing + path back.

🟢 Wallet untouched

Performance restriction pauses bonus accrual only — wallet stays ACTIVE, withdrawals open. Money is not frozen.

🟢 No existing code affected

New enum branch + new mock variants + new labels; existing Exposure/Earnings branches and default flows untouched.

CSP App · Exit + CI + Enforcement design portal · grounded in Enforcement OS v1.10.1, Capability Intervention OS v1.6.1, Exit OS v1.11.1 (all LOCKED) · M1 + M2 of the v1 slice · screenshots captured from the mock build on emulator.
